questions.question: What is sound?
questions.answer: Sound is a form of energy that is produced by vibrating objects and propagated through a medium as longitudinal waves.

questions.question: What is vibration?
questions.answer: Vibration is a rapid to-and-fro motion of an object around its mean position.

questions.question: Name the type of wave in which sound travels.
questions.answer: Sound travels in longitudinal waves.

questions.question: Can sound travel through a vacuum?
questions.answer: No, sound cannot travel through a vacuum because it needs a medium for propagation.

questions.question: Define frequency of sound.
questions.answer: Frequency is the number of vibrations of a sound source per second.

questions.question: Define amplitude of sound.
questions.answer: Amplitude is the maximum displacement of particles from their mean position during vibration.

questions.question: What is pitch of a sound?
questions.answer: Pitch is the property of sound that allows us to distinguish between high and low sounds. It depends on frequency.

questions.question: What is loudness of sound?
questions.answer: Loudness is the measure of energy or intensity of sound that determines how loud or soft a sound is.

questions.question: Define echo.
questions.answer: An echo is a reflected sound that is heard after a delay when sound waves bounce off a surface.

questions.question: What is the speed of sound in air at room temperature?
questions.answer: The speed of sound in air at 20°C is approximately 343 m/s.

questions.question: What is ultrasound?
questions.answer: Ultrasound refers to sound waves with frequency above 20,000 Hz, which is beyond the audible range of humans.

questions.question: What is infrasound?
questions.answer: Infrasound refers to sound waves with frequency below 20 Hz, which is below the audible range of humans.

questions.question: What is the difference between loudness and intensity?
questions.answer: Loudness is a perceptual property of sound, whereas intensity is a physical quantity measuring energy per unit area.

questions.question: What is vibration frequency of human hearing range?
questions.answer: Humans can hear sounds with frequency ranging from 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz.

questions.question: Define resonance.
questions.answer: Resonance is the phenomenon where an object vibrates with maximum amplitude when subjected to a periodic force matching its natural frequency.

questions.question: Name one application of ultrasound in daily life.
questions.answer: Ultrasound is used in medical imaging (sonography) to view internal organs.

questions.question: What is the cause of echo?
questions.answer: Echo is caused due to reflection of sound waves from a hard surface.

questions.question: What is the unit of frequency?
questions.answer: The SI unit of frequency is Hertz (Hz).

questions.question: What type of wave is sound in water?
questions.answer: Sound in water also travels as a longitudinal wave.

questions.question: Give one example of a source of ultrasonic sound.
questions.answer: A dog whistle produces ultrasonic sound.

questions.question: State one difference between audible and ultrasonic sound.
questions.answer: Audible sound is within 20 Hz – 20 kHz; ultrasonic sound is above 20 kHz and cannot be heard by humans.

questions.question: What is the role of air in propagation of sound?
questions.answer: Air acts as a medium that transmits vibrations from the source to the listener.

questions.question: What is the effect of temperature on speed of sound in air?
questions.answer: Speed of sound increases with temperature because air particles move faster at higher temperatures.

questions.question: Define the term 'timbre'.
questions.answer: Timbre is the quality of sound that distinguishes two sounds of the same pitch and loudness.

questions.question: What is the minimum distance required to hear an echo clearly?
questions.answer: Minimum distance for clear echo = 17 m (for speed of sound ≈ 343 m/s) to distinguish original sound from reflected sound.
